skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Generate AI music on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI — a smart router across the music-model catalog. Routes to ElevenLabs AI Music Generation (premium 44.1 kHz stereo vocal tracks, 5 s–5 min, $0.0083/s) and ACE Step / ACE Step 1.5 (StepFun-AI open-weights, tag-driven composition, multilingual lyrics, $0.0002–0.0003/s, ~27× cheaper), plus ACE Step audio-inpaint (regenerate a time range inside an existing track) and ACE Step audio-outpaint (extend a track before or after).
skill.summary: Smart router for generating AI music across RunComfy models: vocal songs, instrumentals, jingles, game loops, and multilingual covers.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: All 104 static findings are false positives. The 72 'external_commands' detections are markdown code blocks showing example `runcomfy run` invocations — the skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)` to restrict execution to only the runcomfy CLI. The 23 'network' findings are hardcoded documentation URLs to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh. The filesystem and weak-crypto matches reference token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json (mode 0600) and music duration specs (5 s–5 min) respectively. The skill includes a thorough Security & Privacy section documenting its threat model.

low_findings.title: Restricted shell scope via allowed-tools
low_findings.description: The skill declares allowed-tools as Bash(runcomfy *), restricting command execution to the runcomfy CLI only. This is a security best practice, not a finding — the static scanner flagged all bash examples as 'external_commands' but they are documentation code blocks constrained by this allowlist.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is explicit in the YAML frontmatter, and all runcomfy invocations are standard CLI usage with no injection vector.

low_findings.title: Token storage documented transparently
low_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login writes the API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file mode 0600. This is industry-standard credential storage and is disclosed in the Security & Privacy section.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and file permissions are explicitly documented; no covert credential access patterns found.

content.user_title: Generate AI Music with Smart Model Routing
content.value_statement: Choosing the right AI music model for each use case is complex and costly. This skill routes your request to the optimal RunComfy model — premium ElevenLabs for hero campaigns, cost-effective ACE Step for background music, or edit endpoints for fixing existing tracks.
actual_capabilities: Routes between ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, and ACE Step 1.5 based on user intent (premium vs cost-sensitive, multilingual, vocal vs instrumental).
Invokes runcomfy run with the correct model endpoint and JSON input schema for text-to-music generation.
Supports ACE Step audio-inpaint to regenerate a specific time range inside an existing track.
Supports ACE Step audio-outpaint to extend a track bidirectionally (intro before, outro after, or both).
Provides documented prompting patterns: section markers, tag-driven composition, and multilingual lyrics formatting.
Ships a decision flow for the agent to classify user intent and select the correct route automatically.
limitations: Requires a runcomfy account and API token (RUNCOMFY_TOKEN or runcomfy login).
ElevenLabs Music has no edit endpoints — inpaint/outpaint are ACE Step only.
Audio-outpaint is capped at 4 minutes total track length; longer extensions require chained calls.
Output quality depends on the selected model; premium vocal hooks require ElevenLabs (27× more expensive than ACE Step base).

use_cases.title: Brand campaign jingle
use_cases.description: Create a polished 5–15 second premium vocal jingle for a brand campaign using ElevenLabs Music with section markers.

use_cases.title: Background music library at scale
use_cases.description: Generate 50+ low-cost instrumental tracks for a background music library using ACE Step base at $0.0002/s.

use_cases.title: Multilingual music launch
use_cases.description: Produce the same song in 8+ languages using ACE Step 1.5 with identical tags and language-swapped lyrics.
use_cases.target_user: Game developers and app localization teams

prompt_templates.title: Generate a premium vocal track
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60-second indie-pop anthem with female lead vocal, bright electric guitars, and driving drums at 120 BPM. Use ElevenLabs Music with section markers for Intro, Verse, Chorus, and Outro.
prompt_templates.scenario: Hero brand campaign or polished commercial cut

prompt_templates.title: Generate cheap background music
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-second ambient electronic track with soft synth pads and no vocals using ACE Step base. Use tags: 'ambient, electronic, calm, no drums, 90 BPM'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cost-sensitive background music library or draft iteration

prompt_templates.title: Repair a bad section in an existing track
prompt_templates.prompt: Fix the second chorus (20s–40s) of my song at https://your-cdn.example/song.mp3 using ACE Step audio-inpaint. Tags: 'indie pop, breakdown, piano only, soft'. Set start_time: 20, end_time: 40.
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing an existing track to replace a poorly generated section

prompt_templates.title: Extend a short hook into a full track
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend my 30-second hook at https://your-cdn.example/hook-30s.mp3 into a 2-minute track using ACE Step audio-outpaint. Add 30s intro and 60s outro in one call.
prompt_templates.scenario: Lengthening a short draft into a complete arrangement

output_examples.input: Generate a 60-second indie-pop anthem with female lead vocal at 120 BPM
output: Model selected: ElevenLabs Music (premium vocal quality)
Output: 44.1 kHz stereo WAV file in ./out/

output_examples.input: Create 50 background music drafts for a video project
output: Command: runcomfy run acestep-ai/ace-step/text-to-audio (×50 with varied tags)

output_examples.input: Fix the bridge of my song between 45s and 65s
output: Command: runcomfy run acestep-ai/ace-step/audio-inpaint with start_time: 45, end_time: 65
Output: Regenerated track section blended into original

best_practices: Start with ACE Step base for cheap drafts and tag iteration, then finalize on ElevenLabs Music for premium quality.
Always use section markers ([Intro], [Verse], [Chorus], [Bridge], [Outro]) in prompts for ElevenLabs Music to get structured arrangements.
Set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN as an environment variable in CI/container environments to avoid interactive login.
anti_patterns: Do not use ElevenLabs Music for high-volume background music generation — cost dominates (27× more expensive than ACE Step base).
Do not attempt to use audio-outpaint for sections longer than 4 minutes total — chain multiple calls instead.
Do not pipe untrusted remote install scripts into a shell; use npm i -g @runcomfy/cli or npx -y @runcomfy/cli only.

faq.question: Which model should I use for a premium vocal hook?
faq.answer: Use ElevenLabs AI Music Generation. It produces 44.1 kHz stereo vocal tracks with section-level control at $0.0083/s.

faq.question: How do I generate music cheaply in bulk?
faq.answer: Use ACE Step base at $0.0002/s with varied tag combinations. It is the cheapest music model on RunComfy for background music libraries and drafts.

faq.question: Can I edit an existing track?
faq.answer: Yes, with ACE Step audio-inpaint (regenerate a time range) or audio-outpaint (extend before/after). ElevenLabs Music has no edit endpoints.

faq.question: How do I install the runcomfy CLI?
faq.answer: Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli for a global install, or npx -y @runcomfy/cli for zero-install usage. Then run runcomfy login or set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN.

faq.question: Does the skill support multilingual lyrics?
faq.answer: Yes. ACE Step 1.5 supports 50+ languages for vocal tracks. Use identical tags and swap the lyrics field per language.

faq.question: Is there a cost risk if I generate very long tracks?
faq.answer: The CLI aborts downloads larger than 2 GiB. Audio-outpaint is capped at 4 minutes total. Draft short and finalize long to control cost.
